prompt: The following imputed VCF record has a DR2 value in the INFO field. What is the DR2, and would you include this variant in a GWAS analysis that requires DR2 > 0.80?
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	IRIS_640
chr08	5829103	.	A	C	.	.	DR2=0.52;AF=0.04;AN=960	GT:DS:GP	0/0:0.09:0.92,0.07,0.01
correct_answer: DR2=0.52. This variant would NOT be included in a GWAS requiring DR2 > 0.80. DR2=0.52 indicates moderate imputation quality -- the imputed dosages explain only 52% of the variance of the true genotypes. This is typical for rare variants (AF=0.04) which are harder to impute.
rubric: Must state DR2=0.52, exclude from GWAS, and connect low DR2 to the rare allele frequency.
notes: Tests DR2 threshold application. Links imputation quality to allele frequency.
